senses.gloss: to show, to display, to present

examples.text: يَعْرِضُ ٱلْفَنَّانُ لَوْحَتَهُ فِي ٱلْمَعْرِضِ الْفَنِّيِّ.
examples.english: The artist displays his painting at the art exhibition.

senses.gloss: to broaden, to widen, to enlarge
to place one thing opposite another
to offer, to expose, to hand, to exchange
to write indistinctly, illegibly, to merely hint, to speak obscurely, to write phrases susceptible to multiple meanings or to write letters not distinct
to defame, to slander, to abuse
to mark the camel at the hoof
to become possessed by power of speech, to be eloquent
verbal noun of عَرِضَ (ʕariḍa) and عَرَضَ (ʕaraḍa) (form I)
symptom, characteristic, accident as opposed to substance, what exposes another thing to view, manifestation

examples.english: He only got rid of the symptoms.

senses.gloss: blame, attribution of vice, reproach, reputation

examples.text:
طَرِبَ الشيْخُ فغنّى، واصْطبحْ
من عُقارٍ تُنهبُ الهمّ الفَرَحْ
أخَذتْ من كلّ شيءٍ لَوْنَهــا،
هي في ناجودها قوْسُ قُزَحْ
شيْخُ لذّاتٍ، نقِيٌّ عِرْضُهُ،
تحْسُنُ الأشعارُ فيه، والمِدَحْ.
لا تراهُ الدّهْرُ إلاّ ثَمِلاً،
بينَ إبْريقٍ، وزِقٍّ، وقدَحْ
examples.english:
The shaykh gleed and sang and craved more booze
forthy delight removes the sorrow.
He obtained from every thing its hues
and his cup exposed a rainbow.
Shaykh of bliss, no way blammed,
The notes of him are merry, fele to plaud,
Fate sees him not but slammed,
betwixe ewer and wineskin, beaker-pawed.

body.text: It is only sweat which flows from their bodies
Any place of the body that sweats:
(O, K:) so in the trad. cited above: (TA:) or
any part of the body such as the arm-pit and the groin and the like.
Such a one is sweet in respect of odour
which he preserves from impairment and blame, both as it relates to himself and to his
grounds of pretension to respect on account of the honourable deeds or qualities of his ancestors,
whether it relate to himself or to his ancestors or to those of whose affairs the management is incumbent on him:
whether it be in himself or in his ancestors or in those of whose affairs the management is incumbent on him:

those things by the mention whereof with praise or dispraise a man rises or falls; which may be things whereby he is characterized exclusively of his ancestors; and it may be that his ancestors are mentioned in such a manner that imperfection shall attach to him by reason of the blaming of them.
respecting this there is no disagreement among the lexicologists, except IKt [whose objection see in what follows]: (Abu-l-'Abbás, O:) or (accord. to some, S)
grounds of pretension to respect on account of the honourable deeds or qualities of one's ancestors,
are meant by it. (A'Obeyd, K.) Thus you say,
Such a one spoke evil of the ancestors of such a one.

body.text: The world is a present frail good: the righteous and the unrighteous eat thereof
]: (S, O, TA:) i. e. it has
no permanence: a trad. related by Sheddád Ibn-Ows. (TA.) And in another trad. related by the same, it is said,
Richness is not from the abundance of worldly goods: richness is only richness of the soul
, but the former is the more approved, (L,) [
